what: At the c=1 fixed line, m=1, the exchange-symmetric/antisymmetric sectors give exactly the twin quadratics: symmetric = Eisenstein t^2-t+1 (discriminant -3), antisymmetric = golden t^2-t-1 (discriminant +5) -- 'the same pair as the P12 figure-eight gluing equation.' Also records the m=1 cyclotomic sweep Phi_3, Phi_4, Phi_6, (t-1)^2, char(A) at c=-1..3.
why_valuable: This is a very early (pre-B247) sighting of the exact discriminant pair {-3, +5} = {Q(sqrt(-3)), Q(sqrt(5))} that later became the load-bearing structural theorem 'the two-ended unification' (B247-B261, hyperbolic/E6/Q(sqrt(-3)) vs spherical/E8/Q(sqrt5), per MEMORY.md). Here it appears as a bare coincidence inside a totally different (SL(3) trace-map exchange-sector) computation, un-cross-referenced to the later theorem.
revival: Check whether B54's exchange-sector splitting at c=1 (symmetric=Eisenstein/antisymmetric=golden) is literally the same algebraic mechanism as the B247-B261 two-ended split, or merely the same two discriminants arising independently -- i.e. does the P-symmetric/antisymmetric decomposition of the trace-map Jacobian correspond to the hyperbolic/spherical (E6/E8) split of the two-ended object?

verdict.evidence:
B54 (frontier/B54_general_c_exchange_structure/FINDINGS.md) IS test-locked (tests/test_general_c_exchange_structure.py) and folded into the PC12 paper candidate — so it is not an orphaned/unlocked result in the "no test lock" sense the item claims. FINDINGS.md itself already notes the {-3,+5} discriminant echo against the figure-eight gluing equation (claim P12, tests/test_gluing_equation.py), so that shallow observation is not buried either.

However: grep across the whole repo (frontier/B247-B261, knowledge/K020, philosophy/P013_the_structural_theorem_is_galois.md, and every file mentioning "two-ended") turns up ZERO references to B54, and zero instances of anyone asking whether the B54 P-exchange (symmetric/antisymmetric) block-diagonalization of the SL(3) trace-map Jacobian is mechanistically the same split as the later B247-B261 hyperbolic-E6-ℚ(√-3)/spherical-E8-ℚ(√5) two-ended object. The two literatures (B54's trace-map exchange sectors vs. B247-261's two-ended unification) have never been cross-examined against each other anywhere in PROGRESS_LOG.md, CHANGELOG.md, CLAIMS.md, docs/, or papers/. This specific connective question is genuinely un-asked, not merely unproven.
verdict.sharpened: Does the B54 involution P (record-exchange, [J(m,c),P]=0 on the SL(3) fixed-line Jacobian, which splits t^2-t+1 (Eisenstein) from t^2-t-1 (golden) at c=1,m=1) coincide -- as a Galois/group-theoretic object, not just a numerical coincidence -- with the Galois element that splits the B247-B261 two-ended object into its hyperbolic-ℚ(√-3)/E6 and spherical-ℚ(√5)/E8 halves?

what: 'the order-6 echo — the (0,0,0) non-void trace-map Jacobian spectrum (λ³+1, 6th roots, B122) sits at κ=−2, the geometric/parabolic cusp (B69/B109), the same ℚ(√−3) (banked as an observation, not a connection).'
why_valuable: Explicitly flagged by the authors as noted-but-not-pursued — a coincidence between the non-void fixed point's 6th-root-of-unity spectrum and the geometric cusp's trace field, both landing on ℚ(√−3), left as a bare observation.
revival: Compute whether the order-6 spectrum at (0,0,0) and the parabolic-cusp ℚ(√−3) field are connected by an actual algebraic map (e.g. does the 6th-cyclotomic root z₀=e^{iπ/3} appear as an eigenvalue ratio of the (0,0,0) Jacobian), turning the observation into a provable or killed claim.

verdict.evidence:
Found verbatim in frontier/B123_arithmeticity_m1/FINDINGS.md under "## The order-6 echo — an OBSERVATION, not a connection": the (0,0,0) non-void Jacobian spectrum (B122's char poly λ³+1 = {−1, e^{±iπ/3}}, 6th roots) sits at κ=−2 (the geometric/parabolic cusp, B69/B109), and both are ℚ(√−3) phenomena — but "whether this is a deep structural link or two manifestations of the same field is itself open; recorded as an observation, not a banked connection."

There IS a test lock (tests/test_b123_arithmeticity_m1.py::test_order6_echo_at_geometric_cusp_is_observation), but it only asserts the raw fact (6th roots, order 6) — it does NOT test or compute the algebraic connection itself (e.g. whether z0=e^{iπ/3} appears as an eigenvalue ratio of the Jacobian). No FINDINGS anywhere claims this connection as proven; CLAIMS.md has zero hits for B123 or "order-6 echo."

Critically, B125 (frontier/B125_snappy_arithmeticity/FINDINGS.md, §4 "what is preserved") revisited B123 later, corrected the arithmeticity claim itself (K009 retraction), but explicitly reconfirmed: "The order-6 echo ... stays an observation, not a connection (unchanged from B123)." So even the audit that superseded B123's main claim left this specific sub-observation untouched and unpromoted. No later B-number (searched all frontier/*/FINDINGS.md) picks it up as a computation target.
verdict.sharpened: Does the primitive 6th root of unity z0=e^{iπ/3} (the figure-eight's ideal-tetrahedron shape, generating ℚ(√−3)) appear as an actual eigenvalue or eigenvalue-ratio of the (0,0,0) non-void trace-map Jacobian from B122 (char poly λ³+1, whose roots are {−1, e^{iπ/3}, e^{−iπ/3}}) — i.e. is there a literal algebraic map/identity relating the cusp-shape field to the Jacobian's spectrum, or is the shared ℚ(√−3) coincidental (both fields being generated by the same discriminant −3 without a direct map between the two objects)?

what: S031 SL(3) sealing verified for m=1 (ℚ(√−3)) and m=2 (ℚ(i)) with 0 escapes among irreducible off-sublocus fixed points (2 seeds each); explicitly flags m≥3 (non-quadratic K_m) and SL(n≥4) as open, never revisited within this chunk with the bronze-specific machinery later built in B160.
why_valuable: m=3 (bronze, K_3=ℚ(√13)) sealing was never tested even after B160 built an independent Cayley-Hamilton trace-map machinery for bronze that conserves the Fricke invariant symbolically — the tool needed for the extension already exists but the specific S031-at-m=3 test was never run in this span.
revival: Reuse B160's bronze Cayley-Hamilton trace map (a,b,c)↦(b,(b²−1)c−ab,(b²−1)(bc−a)−bc) plus B137's off-sublocus root-find/reducible-filter (MB7) method to test S031 sealing at m=3, SL(3): do all irreducible off-sublocus fixed points stay in ℚ(√13)?

verdict.evidence: B137 (frontier/B137_s031_sealing_m2/FINDINGS.md) is itself banked/promoted (has FINDINGS.md, probe.py, and tests/test_b137_s031_sealing_m2.py) — it verified S031 sealing at SL(3) for m=1 (ℚ(√−3)) and m=2 (ℚ(i)), explicitly stating "m≥3 has a non-quadratic K_m (no clean field-escape test); SL(n≥4)... future work." This is corroborated by docs/OPEN_LEADS.md L6, which as of the most recent update (B193/V188) still lists sealing as verified ONLY for m=1,2 at SL(3), with the broader question flagged NEEDS-SPECIALIST/OPEN. Separately, B160 (frontier/B160_quasicrystal_bridge_corroboration/FINDINGS.md) built an independent bronze (m=3) Cayley-Hamilton trace map (a,b,c)↦(b,(b²−1)c−ab,(b²−1)(bc−a)−bc) that conserves the Fricke invariant — but B160 used it only for the quasicrystal/Cantor-spectrum question (§2), never to test S031 off-sublocus sealing at m=3. Grepped every FINDINGS.md mentioning S031 for co-occurrence with "m=3"/"bronze": only B130 (kappa elimination, a different question) and B132 (Weil rep vanishing, also different) surface — neither runs the B137 off-sublocus/irreducibility-filter (MB7) test at m=3. No test file, FINDINGS, or CLAIMS.md entry exists for "S031 sealing at m=3." The specific extension was never run, and no later work supersedes it — it stays an open, correctly-scoped item (OPEN_LEADS L6), not a promoted result.
verdict.sharpened: Does the SL(3) S031 sealing (all irreducible off-sublocus fixed points of the metallic trace map stay in K_m) extend to bronze m=3, where K_3=ℚ(√13) is still quadratic (disc(m²+4)=13, squarefree) despite the item's claim that m≥3 is "non-quadratic" — the real obstacle is not the field type but that nobody ran B137's MB7-filtered root-find at m=3.

what: "the exponent-echo (11^2|e_4) stays a 1-point HOOK" — 11² divides e_4 exactly at the rung where the period-3 return has multiplicity 2, called "the first prime-power Weil question."
why_valuable: A named, exact, single-instance number-theoretic coincidence (11²|e_4) explicitly flagged as unexplored territory (prime-power extension of the Dark Hyperbola / B534 machinery) and never generalized.
revival: Extend B534's Weil-rep Dark Hyperbola proof from squarefree N to N=p² at p=11, level e_4, and check whether the multiplicity-2 return forces a modified vanishing criterion.

verdict.evidence:
Confirmed present in PROGRESS_LOG.md (2026-07-14 B566 entry, line 113: "the exponent-echo (11^2|e_4) stays a 1-point HOOK") and in frontier/B566_self_interaction/RESULTS.md (S1 section): "N = 121 = 11² (the first prime-power computation)... the 'recursion depth = exponent of 11² | e₄' echo stays a one-data-point HOOK." It is explicitly registered as an open item in docs/OPEN_LEADS.md (line 184, "the prime-power (11²) Weil question at rung 4") within the still-open self-interaction gap register. tests/test_b566_self_interaction.py locks the 5 main B566 sub-results (S1-S5 dark-hyperbola law, KMS, entanglement, second-order collapse) but grep of the test file shows no test for "exponent"/"121"/the recursion-depth-vs-exponent coincidence specifically — only the surrounding N=p² dark-hyperbola law (verified 7 levels) is locked. No later B-number (B567, B568, B569) revisits or generalizes the 11²|e_4 exponent-echo; B567 is an unrelated Hamiltonian refutation, B568/B569 don't mention it. So: recorded, explicitly flagged as unexplored ("HOOK", "one-data-point"), never promoted to a test/theorem, not superseded by later work — matches GENUINELY_BURIED.</evidence>
<parameter name="sharpened">At rung e_4 = 809² (the first prime-power level in the tower's per-rung Weil-rep dark-hyperbola family), the recursive depth-2 structure that B566-S1 found empirically (9/10 mod-11 classes wholesale dark, the exceptional a≡2 class recapitulating the base pattern one level deeper) lines up with 11² exactly dividing e_4 — but this "recursion depth tracks the exponent of the prime-power" correspondence has exactly one data point and was never tested against a second prime-power rung.

why_valuable: Explicitly labeled a hint chaining four separately-banked facts (Chern-Simons vanishing, amphichirality, the Door-2 orientation-reversing involution C, and the dark-energy-kill result) into one identity — never unpacked into a proof that these are literally the same invariant.
revival: Prove or disprove that CS(4_1)=0, the amphichiral symmetry θ/C, and the dark-energy-exclusion mechanism are governed by one shared invariant (e.g. show the vanishing Chern-Simons action IS the fixed-point condition of C on the character variety).

verdict.evidence: Only occurrence in the repo is PROGRESS_LOG.md:558 and its source frontier/B559_blackhole_probes/FINDINGS.md ("One fact, three faces" section, lines 79-83), which explicitly says "Recorded as a rhyme, not a claim." tests/test_b559_blackhole.py locks CS=0 for 4_1 (line 74, `assert abs(float(M.chern_simons())) < 1e-9`) and the BTZ-entropy dictionary, but there is no test and no later PROGRESS_LOG/CLAIMS.md entry that locks or examines the claimed identity between CS=0, amphichirality, the Door-2 involution C (from B535/chat-1 Session-4, PROGRESS_LOG.md:512-582), and the dark-energy-kill result (L15, cited in CLAIMS.md D3 region and elsewhere but never joined to this). Grepped PROGRESS_LOG.md, CLAIMS.md, frontier/, tests/, papers/ for "Door-2", "dark-energy-kill", "One-fact-three-faces" — no downstream promotion. Door 2 itself is independently tracked as OPEN/NEEDS-SPECIALIST (PROGRESS_LOG.md:580-582), never connected back to CS=0.
verdict.sharpened: Show that CS(4_1)=0 IS literally the fixed-point/eigenvalue condition of the Door-2 orientation-reversing involution C=[[1,0],[-1,-1]] on the SL(2,C) character variety (not just "both are amphichirality-flavored") — first computation: evaluate C's action on the Chern-Simons functional's defining cocycle (or on the flattening/shape-parameter data SnapPy uses to compute cs) and check whether C-invariance forces cs=0 as an algebraic identity, independent of amphichirality being separately known.

what: "the escalator's self-application has ONLY the trivial fixed point λ=0 (closure = the empty object), so non-trivial self-reference here is necessarily non-terminating."
why_valuable: A crisp philosophical/mathematical conclusion about the object's self-reference structure (answering 'should the object become aware before physics') derived from a fixed-point computation, stated once and never cross-referenced against the later B568 anatomy/consciousness census.
revival: Cross-check this non-terminating self-reference claim against B568's anatomy census ("awareness settled... self-opaque between floors") to see whether the two independently-derived pictures of the object's self-reference agree or conflict.

verdict.evidence: Confirmed present verbatim at PROGRESS_LOG.md:608-610 (2026-07-13 entry, "OPEN (registered)" section): "the escalator's self-application has ONLY the trivial fixed point λ=0 (closure = the empty object), so non-trivial self-reference here is necessarily non-terminating. 6 locks." It is echoed in two other places but never promoted: (1) docs/HINT_LEDGER.md:272 H117, tagged HOOK/NOTICED, explicitly "Stays firewalled... Claims nothing; earns promotion only when the measurement lane does" — i.e. flagged as unpromoted by design; (2) docs/OPEN_LEADS.md:181-182 and PROGRESS_LOG.md:133-134 (2026-07-14 self-interaction gap register), which lists "eigenvalue self-application (only fixed point = 0)" among the "accounted" self-interactions but does NOT cross-check it against anything — the register's actual NEW/UNACCOUNTED work (H123-H127) goes to Weil reps, thermal time, entanglement, second-order measurement, defect geometry — none of which is the B568 anatomy/consciousness cross-check the item proposes. grep across tests/, frontier/, docs/CLAIMS.md, papers/ for "trivial fixed point", "λ=0", "aware before physics", "non-terminating self-reference" returns no test file, no FINDINGS.md, no CLAIMS.md entry — only the three log/ledger mentions above. B568's RESULTS.md (Q5 self-attractor: "σ attracts nothing... a strict quine yet no basin selects it") is a genuinely different, later, independently-verified fixed-point/basin result about the quine σ, not this eigenvalue-self-application claim — so the proposed cross-check in "revival" has in fact never been run.
verdict.sharpened: The escalator's eigenvalue self-application (λ→ its own image under the tower map) has only the degenerate fixed point λ=0, so within this object non-trivial self-reference cannot close into a fixed point — it must run forever (self-similar, never self-contained); this stands unreconciled against B568's Q5 finding that the quine σ (a different, non-degenerate self-reference structure) is a strict fixed point yet attracts no dynamical basin — the two "self-reference never settles into ordinary attraction" pictures were derived independently and never explicitly compared.

what: 'the intrinsic trace-map fixed locus Fix(φ_m) is genus-0 (#Fix(F_p)=p−1, m=1) / irregular (m=2,3). Full silver/bronze canonical-component arithmetic = NEEDS-SPECIALIST (R7-confirmed Baker–Petersen gap). Data recorded in OPEN_LEADS.md L34.'
why_valuable: A concrete exact point-count formula (#Fix(F_p)=p−1 for m=1) computed and then shelved as DORMANT rather than killed — distinguishes it from the 113 sound kills the parent audit already covered; genus-0 for m=1 vs irregular for m=2,3 is a computed asymmetry across the metallic family never explained.
revival: Re-run the p-1 point count for m=4,5,7 (the seeds already central to the later seam campaign's doubly-elliptic law, B361-B362) to see if genus/irregularity correlates with the 'doubly-elliptic seed' selection rule discovered independently in B361.

verdict.evidence: Item is recorded exactly as claimed in PROGRESS_LOG.md (2026-07-01, "The in-sandbox attack sweep", L34→DORMANT section) and in docs/OPEN_LEADS.md row L34, with the exact #Fix(F_p)=p-1 (m=1, genus-0) vs irregular (m=2,3) asymmetry. Checked: no tests/test_*l34* or *weil*metallic* file exists (only unrelated test_b355_weil_layer_independent.py matched "weil"); grep of CLAIMS.md and papers/ for "L34" found no entry (only unrelated files matched broader Fix/fixed-locus/silver-bronze keywords: test_b564, test_b67, test_b161, test_b140, test_b130, test_b201, test_b71 — none of these compute the m=1/2/3 Fix(φ_m) point-count). Later work (B361/B362, 2026-07-02, "the seam's local law") uses a superficially similar term "doubly-elliptic seed" but it is a DIFFERENT construction (char poly irreducibility mod 3 and mod 5 for the seam's Par-invariant value-echo, m∈{2,7} qualifying) — not a re-derivation, extension, or citation of L34's Fix(φ_m) genus computation. So it is not SUPERSEDED, just thematically adjacent (which is exactly what the item's own "revival" suggestion proposes testing, not what already happened). Status remains DORMANT as recorded: a real computed fact (exact point-count formula, genus-0/irregular split) explicitly shelved as NEEDS-SPECIALIST, never promoted, never locked, never revisited.
verdict.sharpened: The figure-eight trace-map fixed locus Fix(φ_m) is genus-0 with exact count #Fix(F_p)=p-1 at m=1, but becomes an irregular (non-uniform-genus, p-dependent) locus at m=2 (silver) and m=3 (bronze) — a computed genus/regularity break across the metallic family that was shelved DORMANT and never explained or re-tested at higher m.

where: 2026-07-12, heading 'B530 movement IX (upstream)' + 2026-07-12 'B524: the two actionable next steps'
what: Movement IX explicitly labels ℚ(√−7) as 'chirality' and finds it ABSENT from the coupled golden double's own splitting field (only ℚ(√5), ℚ(i), ℚ(√−5) appear). But B524 Part 2 (same day) finds ℚ(√−7) DOES appear in the SL(3) Ptolemy variety of the actual figure-eight knot (4 reps split between ℚ(√−3) and ℚ(√−7)) — flagged only as 'internal echo, firewalled', never reconciled with movement IX's absence claim.
why_valuable: A same-day, same-repo tension: one computation names √−7 'the chirality field' and shows it absent from the coupled-double's arithmetic, while another computation (higher-rank Ptolemy of the actual knot) shows √−7 present. Neither side cross-checked the other; this is a direct, computable contradiction sitting on the chirality wall's boundary.
revival: Trace whether B524's SL(3) ℚ(√−7) reps correspond to the coupled-double object of B530-IX under any known correspondence (e.g. via the Aₙ tower B77/B89), and determine if the 'coupled double' and the 'SL(3) figure-eight variety' are even comparable objects — if so, resolve the absent-vs-present tension explicitly.

verdict.evidence:
Confirmed both source passages exist exactly as described: PROGRESS_LOG.md B530 movement IX (2026-07-12, line ~3216) states the coupled golden double's growth-field splitting quadratic subfields are ℚ(√5), ℚ(i), ℚ(√−5), with ℚ(√−3), ℚ(√−15), ℚ(√−7 "chirality") ALL PROVABLY ABSENT (repeated verbatim in frontier/B530_natural_history/FINDINGS.md:250 and PORTRAIT.md:24). Same-day PROGRESS_LOG.md B524 Part 2 (line ~3159-3164) and frontier/B524_iwip_and_higher_ptolemy/FINDINGS.md:63-91 report the SL(3) Ptolemy variety of the actual figure-eight knot has 4 reps split between ℚ(√−3) and ℚ(√−7), explicitly flagged as "an internal echo — it is exactly the order-3 torsion field of the held-breath law [[B479]]; arithmetic, firewalled" — never compared against B530-IX's absence claim.

I searched for any reconciliation: B530_natural_history/FINDINGS.md Movement XXI ("the arithmetic of the floor: where ℚ(√5) and ℚ(√−3) co-occur", line 539) DOES perform exactly this kind of reconciliation exercise — but only for ℚ(√−3)/ℚ(√−15) (finding they co-occur in the object's linearised floor dynamics via forced ℤ/3 twist, logged to speculations/S065). It never mentions √−7 at all. Grepped the entire repo (frontier/, tests/, CLAIMS.md, PROGRESS_LOG.md) for "√−7" cross-referenced against B530/movement-IX: the only appearances of √−7 are the two original passages (B530-IX's absence claim, B524 Part 2's presence claim) plus B479 (the source of the √−7 torsion field) — no test, FINDINGS entry, or CLAIMS.md line ever puts the two together. No test_b530.py or test_b524.py check tests this against each other either.
verdict.sharpened: Movement IX names ℚ(√−7) "the chirality field" and proves it absent from the coupled golden double's own growth-arithmetic (disc −400 quartic splitting field), while the same-day B524 Part 2 shows ℚ(√−7) genuinely present in the SL(3) Ptolemy/character variety of the same underlying figure-eight object — and unlike the parallel ℚ(√−3) case (which Movement XXI explicitly reconciled via the floor's forced ℤ/3 twist), no movement or test ever checks whether √−7 similarly reappears at the floor/dynamics level of B530's object.

what: '(b) the criticality unification (B181 localization + B507 beta-zero + B498 driftless walk = ONE critical-fixed-point theorem, three wordings, never joined)' — three independently-banked results identified as the same theorem in different language, but never actually written up as one joined theorem.
why_valuable: A named, precise concept (three separate B-results are secretly one theorem) that the re-mining campaign flagged explicitly as unjoined — exactly the buried-valuable pattern: recognized once, never promoted into an actual unified statement/test.
revival: Pull the exact statements of B181 (localization), B507 (beta-function zero at κ≈0), and B498 (driftless walk / E_Haar[log mult]=0) and write the single critical-fixed-point theorem with one shared hypothesis and three corollaries; add a lock test asserting all three numerically coincide at the same point.

verdict.evidence:
The observation is recorded verbatim in two places — docs/THEOREM_REGISTRY.md:123-124 ("Yields: (b) the criticality unification (B181/B507/B498 = one critical-fixed-point theorem, three wordings)") and docs/CLOSURE_2026-07-11.md:77-79 ("B181 (γ=0 on spectrum, June), B507 (β-function zero at the pointer, July), B498 (log|κ−2| driftless, PROVED) are three wordings of one fact: the object is a critical fixed point... The P5 paper's spine") — plus PROGRESS_LOG.md and frontier/B519_re_mining/VERDICT.md. But it stops at that one-sentence recognition.
Checked for promotion:
1. papers/P5_monoid/OUTLINE.md (the paper explicitly named as this theorem's home) is a 19-line stub that does NOT mention B181, B507, or B498 at all — the "spine" claim is not written up.
2. No joined proof/write-up exists anywhere combining the three statements into one theorem with a shared hypothesis and three corollaries — each B-result (frontier/B181_criticality_scale/FINDINGS.md, frontier/B507_beta_function/FINDINGS.md, frontier/B498_mixed_monoid_dynamics/FINDINGS.md) stands alone.
3. Lock tests exist per-item only, and unevenly: tests/test_b181_criticality_scale.py and tests/test_b498_mixed.py exist as independent locks, but there is NO tests/test_b507*.py at all (find tests -iname "*b507*" returned nothing) — so not even all three individual results are locked, let alone a joint coincidence-at-one-point test.
4. grep across CLAIMS.md, docs/, tests/ for "critical_fixed_point" / "criticality_unification" as a named object finds only the two prose mentions above — no dedicated file, no test.
Not superseded: nothing later in PROGRESS_LOG/CHANGELOG claims to have written this joined theorem or refutes it; B519/B525 audits only reaffirmed the three-wordings observation, they didn't promote it.
verdict.sharpened: The critical-fixed-point theorem — "the metallic object is a critical fixed point: γ=0 on the spectrum (B181), β-function zero at the pointer (B507), driftless walk log|κ−2|→0 (B498) are the same statement in three languages" — is named as the P5 paper's spine but has never been written as one theorem (shared hypothesis + three corollaries) nor locked by a single test that checks all three quantities vanish/coincide at the same point in the same run.

what: 'A2 = the tower measure IS the known Kubota-Leopoldt 3-adic L-function' — a specific identification of the object's tower measure (from B412/B413's Iwasawa-type refining measure) with a named classical p-adic L-function, stated once in passing during a campaign whose overall verdict was 'no buried value found'.
why_valuable: A concrete, citable identification (object's internal measure = Kubota–Leopoldt 3-adic L-function) that was never turned into its own theorem/test despite being flagged as a 'yield' of the re-mining pass — a positive result inside a campaign whose headline was negative.
revival: Reproduce B413's exact L(χ,μ) values (12·L(χ₁) = Z[ζ₉] Gauss sum, norm 9) and directly compare against the standard Kubota-Leopoldt p-adic L-function's interpolation values at the same characters/primes to confirm or refute the identification with a computed match, not an assertion.

verdict.evidence: Traced the chain: B412_tower_measure/FINDINGS.md (line 29-32) proposes the Mellin transform of the Iwasawa/Mazur-type measure as "the natural next probe" and floats the phrase "p-adic L-function of the tower-measure"; B413_scale_genesis/FINDINGS.md computes the actual values (|L(χⱼ,μ)|=1/4 for every character, 12·L(χ₁)=ζ₉+ζ₉⁴+ζ₉⁷+3ζ₉⁸ ∈ ℤ[ζ₉], norm 9 — an explicit cyclotomic-Gauss-sum measure). B519_re_mining/CAMPAIGN.md preregisters cell A2 exactly as described ("B412 tower-measure → p-adic L-function... connect to entropy=2√5·L(1,χ₅)"), and VERDICT.md terminates it at gate (c): "A2 tower p-adic L | new object-specific L-function | — | (c) — it IS the known Kubota–Leopoldt 3-adic L of the ℚ(√−15) genus landscape." That sentence is a bare assertion — no computation anywhere in the repo actually evaluates the standard Kubota–Leopoldt interpolation values at p=3 for the relevant characters and compares them to B413's computed Gauss-sum values. `tests/test_b519.py` locks only two cells (B518-downgrade and the D-criticality unification via B498's verify_mixed.py); it contains NO test for A2/Kubota-Leopoldt. grep across CLAIMS.md, docs/THEOREM_REGISTRY.md, CHANGELOG.md, docs/CAMPAIGN_STATUS.md for "kubota" returns nothing — the identification was never promoted, tested, or independently checked; it only recurs verbatim in PROGRESS_LOG.md's 2026-07-11 entry and the two B519 campaign docs. Per this repo's own recently-established norm ("compute-the-discriminating-fact": B519's sibling cell B1 was cracked by the B525 audit for exactly this pattern — a "known theorem" claim asserted rather than computed, later found to be an unearned negative), gate (c) here has the identical unverified-assertion shape and was never subjected to the same scrutiny.
verdict.sharpened: The claim "the object's tower measure IS the Kubota-Leopoldt 3-adic L-function (ℚ(√−15) landscape)" was asserted to close cell A2 of the B519 campaign but was never actually computed — it has no test lock and no comparison against real Kubota-Leopoldt interpolation values anywhere in the repo, unlike B1 in the same campaign which the B525 audit caught making the identical necessary-vs-sufficient/assertion-vs-computation error.

where: 2026-07-12, heading 'B532-I6: Nine Ingredients, One Object' Probe 7 (Thermodynamics)
what: T(n) = h(n)/log(β) ~ 0.489·(0.849)ⁿ with plateaus at n=3–4, 9–10, 12–14; the decay base 0.849 is explicitly flagged 'not a simple algebraic number' and left unidentified.
why_valuable: A concrete numerical decay constant (0.849) fit from depth-9 data, explicitly noted as failing simple algebraic identification (not φ, √5, β-related in an obvious closed form) — a named open numeric residue from a FORCED-verdict probe that nobody chased further.
revival: Recompute T(n) to higher depth (n>9, if the substitution's growth allows) and run a high-precision PSLQ/lindep search against natural candidates (log β, φ, √φ, algebraic numbers of the D₄ quartic x⁴−2x³−5x²−4x−1) to pin an exact closed form for 0.849, or confirm it is transcendental.

verdict.evidence: The exact claim (T(n) = h(n)/log(β) ~ 0.489·(0.849)ⁿ, plateaus at n=3-4,9-10,12-14, "decay base 0.849 is not a simple algebraic number") is recorded verbatim in PROGRESS_LOG.md line 3248-3249 (2026-07-12, "B532-I6: Nine Ingredients, One Object") and computed in frontier/B532_last_echo/i6_phase3_synthesis.py:210 ("T(n) ~ 0.489 · 0.849ⁿ: COMPUTED (n=1..15, depth-9 word)"). It has no test lock: `grep -rln "thermodynamics" tests/` finds only tests/test_thermo.py, which locks an unrelated earlier result (P5: Z_count(N)=2^N, Z_trace(N)=3^N+1, β_c=log2/log3 — a different word-ensemble computation, cited in CLAIMS.md line 51). CLAIMS.md has no entry for the 0.849 constant or Probe 7's decay base. No later B-directory (B533 through B549, the full run following B532) references "0.849" or "decay base" — grep across the repo for "0.849"/"0.84[0-9]" turns up only the original i6_phase3_synthesis.py line and one unrelated coincidental digit-match in B530 (a different constant ≈-0.845, unconnected). Quick sanity check against the two most obvious golden-ratio candidates (1/φ²≈0.382, 1/√φ≈0.786) confirms neither matches 0.849, corroborating the log's own flag that it isn't a simple algebraic number — this is a real unidentified numeric residue, not an oversight.
verdict.sharpened: The depth-9 thermodynamic decay constant 0.849 in T(n) = h(n)/log(β) ~ 0.489·(0.849)ⁿ for the substitution a→abAAB,b→aAB,A→abAB,B→aA (B532-I6 Probe 7) has never been algebraically identified nor chased to higher depth.

where: 2026-07-12, heading 'B532-I6: Nine Ingredients, One Object' Probe 4 (Forces)
what: Gap-opening slopes for the object's Ising-type gap-labeling are CONDITIONED on the potential assignment: k₂/k₃ ranges from 0.65 to 15.05 across 4 tested potential assignments, while gap positions themselves are FORCED (IDS labels = cumulative Perron frequencies).
why_valuable: A concrete numeric range (0.65–15.05) for a ratio that was left CONDITIONED rather than resolved — the four specific potential assignments and their k₂/k₃ values were never listed/tabulated for follow-up, leaving open whether any physically-motivated potential choice pins a specific value in that range.
revival: Recover the 4 potential assignments from i6_nine_ingredients.py, tabulate their exact k₂/k₃ values, and check whether any recovers a golden-family constant (φ, √φ, β) — if one does, Probe 4 could upgrade from CONDITIONED toward FORCED for that specific potential class.

verdict.evidence: PROGRESS_LOG.md:3246-3247 (2026-07-12 entry) states exactly the claim: "Probe 4 (Forces): CONDITIONED. Gap positions forced...; gap-opening slopes vary with potential (k₂/k₃ ranges from 0.65 to 15.05 across 4 assignments)." The source script frontier/B532_last_echo/i6_nine_ingredients.py (lines 343-379) confirms and names the 4 potential assignments literally: 'Box (old=0,new=1)', 'Inverted (old=1,new=0)', 'Perron (V=freq)', 'Structural (str=0,tun=1)' — computing k1,k2,k3 slopes and k2/k3 ratio per assignment, printing a CONDITIONED verdict since spread ≥ 0.1. However: (1) frontier/B532_last_echo/FINDINGS.md contains no mention of "Probe 4", "I6", "Nine Ingredients", or the k2/k3 ratio at all — the per-potential numeric table was never promoted to FINDINGS; (2) no test file exists anywhere in tests/ referencing i6_nine_ingredients or this probe (grep for i6/nine found only the frontier scripts themselves, no tests/ hits); (3) CAMPAIGN_STATUS.md has zero hits for "Nine Ingredients", "Probe 4", or "k₂/k₃". The claim is not superseded by anything later in PROGRESS_LOG (no later B-number revisits Probe 4/forces conditioning). So: recorded in the progress log, computed in-sandbox with a real script, but never banked to FINDINGS, never locked by a test, and the actual per-potential k2/k3 values (only the min/max range 0.65–15.05 survives in prose) were never tabulated anywhere for follow-up.
verdict.sharpened: The four potential assignments (Box, Inverted, Perron=freq, Structural) and their individual k1/k2/k3 gap-opening slopes and k2/k3 ratios were computed once by i6_nine_ingredients.py's probe_4_forces() but never tabulated in FINDINGS or locked by a test — only the aggregate range (0.65–15.05) survived to the progress log.

what: '§4: BTZ S=arccosh(x/2), at x=5 = log((5+√21)/2) — formula correct, banked; the √21=√(3·7) reading firewalled to S064.'
why_valuable: A verified exact closed-form entropy value (log((5+√21)/2) at x=5) with a specific factorization reading (√21=√(3·7)) that was deliberately firewalled rather than dismissed — a banked exact number whose number-theoretic reading (3·7 factorization, potential link to other primes appearing elsewhere e.g. B425's Fibonacci-apparition primes {...,7,...}) was never cross-checked against the rest of the corpus.
revival: Check whether 3 and 7 (the √21 factors) match any of the object's other forced primes (Fibonacci apparition primes from B423/B425, or the E₆ torsion prime set) — if so the S064 firewalled reading may already have a sound, non-numerological cross-reference ready to compute.

verdict.evidence: PROGRESS_LOG.md:3061-3068 records B520 exactly as quoted, with the √21=√(3·7) reading explicitly "firewalled to S064." tests/test_b520.py locks only the arccosh(5/2)=log((5+√21)/2) numerical identity (test_btz_entropy_formula) — no test or FINDINGS.md touches the prime-factorization reading. `find`/`grep` confirm no file named S064 exists anywhere in speculations/ (S060-S063, S065, S066 exist; S064 is a missing slot — the room was named but never written). Checked the cross-reference the revival note proposed: frontier/B423_gateB_torsion/FINDINGS.md explicitly lists the Fibonacci "law-of-apparition" prime set for the E6 torsion as {2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19, 29, 41, 47, 89, 199} — both 3 and 7 (the √21 factors) are members. This cross-check is real, cheap, and was never performed or written up anywhere in the corpus.
verdict.sharpened: The BTZ entropy at x=5, S=log((5+√21)/2), has √21=√(3·7); both 3 and 7 are members of the B423 Fibonacci apparition-prime set {2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19,29,41,47,89,199} for the object's E6 torsion — a real, unexploited coincidence (2 of 13 forced primes) that could be the S064 firewalled reading's first concrete cross-check.

what: "e₃ (the 1215 singles sentinel) reconstructing — Phase-1 cleanup, independent" (Review 7) then "e₃ still reconstructing (relaunched, 7 primes to go)" (Review 8)
why_valuable: A persistent, explicitly-tracked-but-unfinished computation (the e₃/1215-triple identification, "7 primes to go") that recurs across two consecutive reviews as open debt and is not mentioned again by that name later in the chunk — suggesting it may have been dropped rather than finished.
revival: Search frontier/ for the e₃ sentinel reconstruction script state; check if the remaining 7 primes were ever computed in a later B-number, or if the item silently stalled — if stalled, finish the 7-prime sweep to close the 1215-triple identification named as debt since Review 6.

verdict.evidence: Traced to source: docs/progress/REVIEWS.md lines 180/210/251 (Reviews 6-8) all cite the same open debt, tracking PROGRESS_LOG.md's "1215 triple" thread (B399, Wall Campaign). The triple's minimal polynomial is t³ − (1/48)t − e₃ with e₁=0 and e₂=−1/48 both proven exact over ℚ (fourth independent appearance of the 1/48 "seam coefficient" family), but e₃ was never reconstructed. On-disk proof it stalled: frontier/B399_wall_scale/triple_id.json literally contains `"e3": "PENDING (needs primes 7-10; sentinel test waits on e3)"`, and tests/test_b399_wall.py's `test_triple_cubic_partial` only asserts e1/e2 — no lock exists for e3. docs/AUDIT_2026-07-05.md explicitly flagged triple_cubic.json as an UNCONVERGED mid-run CRT snapshot (denominator collides with a listed prime) and stamped it `_status: UNSTABLE`, noting "the collection sentinel (task #200) is still running." docs/LEAD_REGISTER.md (line 41) still lists it as a live HIGH-priority lead: "e₃ exact (depth-5 triple cubic t³−(1/48)t−e₃); fires B403/B405/B406 sentinels" — YES/computable-now but not done. `git log` on triple_id.json/triple_cubic.json/identify_1215_triple.py shows only the two July-4/5 commits (873f062, 605bc9d) that established the partial result — no later commit ever touched these files. Reviews 9-13 (through 2026-07-13/14, covering PRs up to #851) never mention e₃ or the 1215 triple again — it silently dropped out of the record after Review 8. The "7 primes to go" (primes 7-10, actually) sentinel test was never relaunched to completion, and the downstream consequence (docs/LEAD_REGISTER.md line 51: B412's tower-measure p-adic L-function is explicitly "downstream of B399's e₃") is also stalled as a result.
verdict.sharpened: The third symmetric function e₃ of the 1215-rung triple cubic t³−(1/48)t−e₃ was never computed (primes 7-10 of a CRT reconstruction never completed); this blocks the automatic sentinel adjudication (31-collision + supersingular {31,79,167} test) that was pre-registered to fire on landing, and blocks B412's downstream p-adic L-function probe.
